message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

[phaseprompt] Read ui-audit/02-comparison.md, ui-audit/03-verification.md, and ui-audit/03c-pre-reconciliation.md. Apply corrections and additions from both 03 and 03c to 02-comparison.md.

Do not re-read source code. Trust the verified corrections in 03 and 03c. Do not introduce any new claims that are not already in 02, 03, or 03c.

Apply corrections in this order.

First, apply the eleven original discrepancies from 03-verification.md to the appropriate rows in 02-comparison.md. For each, update the cell content to reflect the corrected claim.

Second, apply the three additional discrepancies from 03c-pre-reconciliation.md section three. These are tokens currently described in 02 with hedged language like "may or may not be registered" or "requires verification" but are actually defined in index.css. Replace the hedged language with the confirmed-registered status and the index.css line reference.

Third, add the four shared CSS files from 03c-pre-reconciliation.md section two as new entries in 02-comparison.md. Use the category assignments recorded in 03c. Because these are shared infrastructure rather than module-specific findings, add them as a new column or section labeled "Shared infrastructure" within each affected category table, rather than placing them under Console, Designer, or Settings. The five fields from 03c map directly to the five-field structure already used in the comparison tables.

For operationalState.css specifically, preserve the framing from 03c: the hardcoded ISA-101 colors with !important are intentional design, not an inconsistency. Add this as an explicit note in the relevant entry so the recommendations phase reads it as documented exception, not a gap to close.

For lod.css, add it under category 10 with a note that it is structural-only CSS with no deviations — recorded for completeness so the comparison is not silently missing it.

Fourth, add the four missing-from-comparison entries from 03-verification.md if they overlap with the shared files in 03c, reconcile to a single entry per file rather than duplicating. If any missing-from-comparison entry from 03 is not covered by 03c, add it on its own using whatever information 03 provides.

After all updates, append a section at the bottom of 02-comparison.md titled reconciliation-log. List every change made with reference to which source justified it: 03-verification entry number, 03c section and item, or both. The log should make it possible to trace any reconciled claim back to its evidence.

Important: do not edit the per-module audit files 01-console.md, 01-designer.md, or 01-settings.md. They will remain with their original (some incorrect) content. The comparison file is now the corrected source of truth for downstream phases. Note this in the reconciliation log: "Per-module audit files not updated; 02-comparison.md is the authoritative source post-reconciliation."

When done print: count of original discrepancies applied, count of additional discrepancies applied, count of shared files added, count of total reconciliation-log entries, and stop.

addedBlocks:
## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- audit: Verify a spec unit's implementation against its authoritative spec. Reads the manifest, reads spec files, walks target code, catalogs every gap, and writes task files for each finding.
- spec: Read the implementation spec for a given I/O module. Usage: /spec <module>. Modules: console, process, designer, graphics, display-elements, shapes, opc
- design-qa: Inventory all implementations of a cross-cutting feature across the codebase, surface inconsistencies, ask targeted design questions, and write the answers as an authoritative decision file. Run this BEFORE /audit on any feature marked ⚠️ NOT SPECCED in the manifest.
- spec-index: Print a quick-reference index of every audit unit — unit ID, canonical name, wave, and current task status. Use this to look up the correct unit ID or module name before running spec-scout or feature-agent.
- agent-guide: Print a reference guide for all custom agents in this project — what each one does, when to use it, how to launch it, and usage examples.
- wrapup: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
- review: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
- deepreview: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
- summarize: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Reconcile UI audit comparison with verification findings

content.text: Reading all three source files before making any edits.

content.content:
1	# UI Audit Comparison — Cross-Module Analysis
2	
3	**Source files:** `ui-audit/01-console.md`, `ui-audit/01-designer.md`, `ui-audit/01-settings.md`
4	**Date:** 2026-05-27
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Category 1 — Color Palette and Theme Tokens
9	
10	| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
11	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
12	| **Implementation** | Shared-component; all colors via CSS custom properties from `index.css`; no module-specific token overrides | Inline-styles; all color references via CSS custom properties from app-shell token registry; no module-specific token declarations | Inline-styles; all color references are CSS custom property calls into app-shell token set defined in `index.css`; no module-specific token definitions | All three: primary color infrastructure is the 138-token registry in `index.css`; no module owns its own token namespace | — |
13	| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.css:19–295` (138 tokens); hardcoded values in `AlarmListPane.tsx`, `PointTablePane.tsx`, `TrendPane.tsx`, `index.tsx` | `index.css:19–219`; `shared/theme/tokens.ts` (JS mirror); hardcoded overrides in `DesignerToolbar`, `DesignerTabBar`, `DesignerStatusBar`, `DesignerGraphicsList` | `index.css:17–219`; `settingsStyles.ts:1–71` (subset of token aliases); hardcoded overrides in `Users.tsx`, `Roles.tsx`, `Groups.tsx`, `OpcSources.tsx`, `BulkUpdate.tsx` | `index.css` is the canonical token registry for all three | — |
14	| **Visual properties actually applied** | ~20 token references; hardcoded hex for alarm priority/quality badge colors, TrendPane series palette, published dot | ~15 token references; 9 hardcoded color values in shell components; 2 undefined tokens (`--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`) | ~15 token references including `--io-border-subtle`, `--io-success`, `--io-status-fg`; undefined `--io-text-on-accent` in `btnPrimary`; hex-alpha badge concatenation bug in OpcSources | All three use `var(--io-accent)`, `var(--io-border)`, `var(--io-text-primary)`, `var(--io-text-muted)`, `var(--io-surface)` family, `var(--io-success)`/`--io-warning`/`--io-danger` | — |
15	| **Deviations from app shell** | `var(--io-bg)` and `var(--io-text)` referenced but not defined in `index.css`; alarm priority badge colors hardcoded instead of using `--io-alarm-*` tokens; published dot `#10b981` ≠ `var(--io-success)` | `var(--io-surface-hover)` and `var(--io-font-sans)` undefined; 9 hardcoded values bypass tokens; `#09090b` hardcoded for active IconBtn text instead of `var(--io-accent-foreground)` | `--io-text-on-accent` undefined in `btnPrimary` across ~15 files; hex-alpha CSS var concatenation bug (`${color}20`) renders OpcSources StatusBadge backgrounds as invalid; hardcoded modal overlay/shadow values in Users/Roles/Groups | All three reference at least one undefined token; all three have hardcoded hex colors that bypass the token system | Each module references a different set of undefined tokens |
16	| **Notes** | Console quality badge colors (`#10B981`, `#F59E0B`, `#EF4444`) have no corresponding tokens; hardcoded is the only option for that sub-type | `DesignerGraphicsList` `SCOPE_COLORS`/`MODE_COLORS` are fully hardcoded rgba objects with no registry equivalent | OpcSources StatusBadge `${color}20` concatenation bug is a functional regression: backgrounds render as transparent in all browsers | — | — |
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Category 2 — Typography
21	
22	| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
23	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
24	| **Implementation** | Inline-styles; no Console-specific CSS classes or shared typography component | Inline-styles; no module-local CSS; `FieldLabel` component in `DesignerRightPanel` is the only reusable label primitive in any Designer file | Inline-styles; no module-specific CSS; `settingsStyles.ts:labelStyle` exports a named label constant used across most pages | All three use inline-styles exclusively; no shared typography component exists across modules | — |
25	| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.css:179–218` (16 scale tokens + `--io-font-mono`); inline styles scattered across `index.tsx`, `PaneWrapper`, `ConsolePalette`, `PaneConfigModal`, `AlarmListPane` | `index.css:178–218` (same 16 tokens); `DesignerRightPanel:201–217` (`FieldLabel`); `DesignerModeTabs`, `DesignerToolbar`, `DesignerTabBar`, `DesignerStatusBar` | `index.css`; `index.tsx:198–224` (nav group/item); `SettingsPageLayout:36–55` (page title); `settingsStyles.ts:15–21` (labelStyle); `SettingsTabs:60–75` (tab labels) | `index.css` typography token scale is the reference for all three; none actually use it | — |
26	| **Visual properties actually applied** | Raw px integers 9–18px; `fontFamily: monospace` on alarm tag/time (not `--io-font-mono`); SubGroupLabel at 9px and PaneTypeBadge at 10px are below smallest token (11px) | Raw px integers 9–20px; `var(--io-font-mono)` used correctly for zoom input (only instance); `var(--io-font-sans)` referenced in zoom dropdown but undefined; RightPanel `FieldLabel`: 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em | Raw px integers 11–18px; bare `"monospace"` keyword in `Groups.tsx` (not `--io-font-mono`); `labelStyle`: 12px/500/`--io-text-secondary`; page title 18px/600; table headers 11px/600/uppercase | All three use raw pixel integer literals; none reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`); all inherit the browser sans-serif stack | — |
27	| **Deviations from app shell** | Zero typography scale tokens used; `fontFamily: monospace` instead of `--io-font-mono`; SubGroupLabel 9px below minimum token; no font sizes map cleanly to token equivalents | Zero typography scale tokens used; `var(--io-font-sans)` undefined; five different `letterSpacing` values for uppercase labels within `DesignerRightPanel` alone (0em, 0.04em, 0.05em, 0.06em, 0.08em) | Nav group header 11px vs AppShell 10px (1px drift); three different `letterSpacing` values across semantically identical uppercase label elements (0.06em, 0.07em, 0.08em); bare `"monospace"` in `Groups.tsx` instead of `--io-font-mono` | All three: zero typography scale tokens used; all three use bare `monospace` or no font-family token for monospace contexts | — |
28	| **Notes** | None of the inline sizes map cleanly to token equivalents (e.g., 15px, 16px are not defined tokens) | `FieldLabel` (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em) is the only reusable typography primitive in any of the three modules | Typography is most internally consistent in Settings; deviations are minor relative to Console and Designer | — | — |
29	
30	---
31	
32	## Category 3 — Toolbars
33	
34	| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
35	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
36	| **Implementation** | Module-local-component; single 48px horizontal header bar defined as inline JSX inside `ConsolePage` | Module-local-component; four stacked dedicated components (`DesignerModeTabs` 36px, `DesignerToolbar` 44px, `DesignerTabBar` 36px, `DesignerStatusBar` 28px) | **Absent.** Per-page action rows above tables are inline flexbox rows, not toolbars. No toolbar container, no icon-button strip found | N/A — Settings has no toolbars; Console and Designer both use module-local components with no shared toolbar primitive | — |
37	| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.tsx:1942–2826` (full header + tab strip); `index.tsx:247–466` (`LayoutPickerButton`) | `DesignerModeTabs:207–217`; `DesignerToolbar:1159–1169`; `DesignerTabBar:363–376`; `DesignerStatusBar:192–203` | N/A | — | — |
38	| **Visual properties actually applied** | 48px height; `background: var(--io-surface)`; `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; active tab: `borderBottom: 2px solid var(--io-accent)`; tab labels 13px/600 active/400 inactive; separator dividers `width:1, height:18, background: var(--io-border)` | DesignerToolbar 44px: same bg/border pattern; `IconBtn` 32×32 `var(--io-radius)`, `transition: background 0.1s, color 0.1s`; mode/tab strips with 2px accent underline; StatusBar 28px at bottom with segment dividers | N/A | Console + Designer share: `background: var(--io-surface)`, `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, active tab underline `2px solid var(--io-accent)`, tab labels font-weight active/inactive distinction | — |
39	| **Deviations from app shell** | `var(--io-surface)` background where app shell topbar uses `var(--io-surface-primary)` (darker); none of 6 `--io-btn-*` tokens used; no toolbar height token; Publish button rendered twice in right controls block (duplicate render) | Toolbar text action buttons use `borderRadius: 6` integer while IconBtn in the same component uses `var(--io-radius)` (both 6px currently but inconsistent); all heights hardcoded (36/44/36/28); loading skeleton uses 40px instead of actual 44px | N/A | Console + Designer: all toolbar heights are hardcoded integers with no token | — |
40	| **Notes** | `LayoutPickerButton` is module-local with its own custom inline absolute-positioned dropdown (`zIndex: 200`) | Loading skeleton toolbar placeholder uses 40px height vs the actual 44px, producing a brief layout shift on load | N/A | — | — |
41	
42	---
43	
44	## Category 4 — Menus
45	
46	| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
47	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
48	| **Implementation** | Mix; right-click context menus use `shared/components/ContextMenu`; export quick-format dropdown is a hand-rolled inline-positioned panel | Mix; three patterns: custom inline dropdown (File menu, `position: absolute`), custom fixed-position dropdown (zoom presets, `getBoundingClientRect`), shared `ContextMenu` (tab right-click) | Shared-component only; `ContextMenu` is the sole menu type; no custom dropdown panels | All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` for right-click context menus | — |
49	| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.tsx:3108–3405` (right-click menus); `index.tsx:2591–2654` (export dropdown); `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | `DesignerModeTabs:317–389` (File menu); `DesignerToolbar:1373–1469` (zoom dropdown); `DesignerTabBar:102–253` (tab context menu); `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx`; `Users:1628–1660`, `Roles`, `Groups` | `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | — |
50	| **Visual properties actually applied** | ContextMenu: delegated to shared component; Export dropdown: `position: absolute`, `background: var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `border: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: 6`, `boxShadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)`, items 13px | ContextMenu: delegated to shared component; File menu: `position: absolute`, `var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `boxShadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.35)`, `zIndex: 300`; Zoom dropdown: `position: fixed` (anchored via `getBoundingClientRect`), `zIndex: 2000` | ContextMenu container: `var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `border: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius)`, `boxShadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)`, `zIndex: 1800`; items: `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger (not `--io-danger`) | All three: ContextMenu uses `var(--io-surface-elevated)` bg, `var(--io-border)` border, `var(--io-radius)` radius; hover applied via imperative `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` DOM mutation | — |
51	| **Deviations from app shell** | Export dropdown uses direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.background`) for hover rather than shared `ContextMenu`; no CSS class or React state | Zoom dropdown hover uses `var(--io-surface-hover)` (undefined token — no visual feedback); zoom font uses `var(--io-font-sans)` (undefined); box-shadows differ between File menu and zoom dropdown and do not use `--io-shadow-lg`; `zIndex: 300` vs `zIndex: 2000` across two custom dropdowns | None; Settings consumes `ContextMenu` identically to other modules | All three: custom dropdown implementations (where present) do not use `--io-shadow-lg` token | ContextMenu uses `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger items rather than `var(--io-danger)`; same numeric value in dark theme but diverges in light theme |
52	| **Notes** | Export dropdown backdrop uses `position: fixed` — safe here since toolbar is not inside an RGL transform ancestor | File menu has no Escape key handler; Zoom dropdown uses `position: fixed` to escape toolbar's potential transform context (consistent with CLAUDE.md invariant) | `ConfirmDialog` (shared component) is used correctly for destructive actions; no dropdown menus needed | — | — |
53	
54	---
55	
56	## Category 5 — Side Panels
57	
58	| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
59	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
60	| **Implementation** | Module-local-component; `ConsolePalette.tsx` with `useConsolePanelResize` hook; 220px default width, resizable by drag | Module-local-component; four files (`DesignerLeftPalette`, `DesignerRightPanel`, `ShapePointSelector`, `PointPickerModal`); no shared panel primitive | Module-local-component; bespoke `<aside>` in `index.tsx`; not derived from app-shell sidebar infrastructure | None; all three implement side panels independently | — |
61	| **Source-of-truth files** | `ConsolePalette.tsx:52–537`; `index.tsx:1127–1132` (state); `useConsolePanelResize.ts` | `DesignerLeftPalette:2430–2438`; `DesignerRightPanel:5961–5992`; `ShapePointSelector:378–393`; `PointPickerModal:44–55` | `index.tsx:179–229` (aside); `AppShell.tsx:1327–1516` (comparison baseline) | — | — |
62	| **Visual properties actually applied** | 220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`; `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; accordion section headers 36px height; section label 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; `--io-text-muted`; resize handle 5px `ns-resize` | DesignerLeftPalette: `background: var(--io-surface)`, `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; `SectionHeader` 11px/600/uppercase/0.06em; 4px resize handle; palette tiles `var(--io-surface-elevated)` bg; DesignerRightPanel: `background: var(--io-surface)`, `borderLeft: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; RightPanel TabBar tabs 10px/uppercase | 220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`; `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; nav items `padding: 7px 10px`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius)`; active: `var(--io-accent-subtle)` bg + `var(--io-accent)` text/color | Console + Settings: 220px width, `var(--io-surface-secondary)` bg, `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)` | DesignerLeftPalette diverges: uses `var(--io-surface)` not `var(--io-surface-secondary)` |
63	| **Deviations from app shell** | 220px vs `--io-sidebar-width: 240px`; `var(--io-text)` undefined in section search input; view mode selector hover via DOM mutation (`borderRadius: 3` vs `var(--io-radius)` on list items); `--io-sidebar-bg` token not used | DesignerLeftPalette: equipment tiles use `var(--io-surface-sunken)` vs `var(--io-surface-elevated)` for other tile types; drag ghost hardcodes `borderRadius: 4px` and `color: "#09090b"`; `CanvasLayerRow` emoji icons (👁/🔒) vs SVG elsewhere; `var(--io-surface-raised)` undefined for `RowSection`; all hover via DOM mutation | 220px vs `--io-sidebar-width: 240px`; active left-border accent missing (AppShell shows `2px solid var(--io-accent)` on active items; Settings shows none); no hover transition (AppShell transitions bg/color); group header 11px vs AppShell 10px | Console + Settings: same width deviation (220px vs 240px token) and same background token (`var(--io-surface-secondary)`) but neither uses `--io-sidebar-width` | — |
64	| **Notes** | `PointsBrowserPanel` (app-shell) embedded inside the palette Points section; its styles are app-shell-owned | Stencil tile uses ⬜ emoji placeholder thumbnail; equipment tiles use `ShapeThumbnail` SVG; display element tiles use `DisplayElementPreview` SVG — three thumbnail strategies | Missing active left-border accent is the most visible deviation from AppShell nav pattern | — | — |
65	
66	---
67	
68	## Category 6 — Buttons
69	
70	| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
71	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
72	| **Implementation** | Inline-styles; all native `<button>` elements; no shared Button component | Mix; no shared `<Button>` component; five patterns: primary (filled accent), secondary (bordered), destructive, `IconBtn` primitive, toggle/chip; `IconBtn` is the only reusable button primitive | Module-local-component; four named variants in `settingsStyles.ts` (`btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall`); row action buttons in `Users.tsx` duplicated inline | All three use `var(--io-accent)` for primary action button backgrounds; all three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` for secondary/outline borders; none use the 6 `--io-btn-*` tokens | — |

139	
140	## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules
141	
142	1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
143	2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
144	3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
145	4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
146	5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
147	6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
148	7. **`--io-input-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the five `--io-input-bg`, `--io-input-border`, `--io-input-focus-border`, `--io-input-placeholder`, `--io-input-height` tokens defined in `index.css`.
149	8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
150	9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
151	10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
152	11. **No `--io-modal-*` tokens used.** All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`, the three modal-specific tokens defined in `index.css`.
153	12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.
154	
155	---
156	
157	## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules
158	
159	1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
160	2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including two undefined tokens (`--io-modal-backdrop`, `--io-overlay`) with no consistent value among the three.
161	3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
162	4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
163	5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
164	6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.
165	7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-info`, `--io-space-*`, `--io-accent-rgb`, `--io-surface`.
166	8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
167	9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
168	10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
169	11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — all uncoordinated with the `--io-z-modal: 300` token.
170	12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.
171	
172	---
173	
174	## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on
175	
176	1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.
177	
178	2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.
179	
180	3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.
181	
182	4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.
183	
184	5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.
185	
186	6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.
187	
188	7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.
189

# UI Audit Comparison — Cross-Module Analysis

**Source files:** `ui-audit/01-console.md`, `ui-audit/01-designer.md`, `ui-audit/01-settings.md`
**Date:** 2026-05-27

---

## Category 1 — Color Palette and Theme Tokens

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Shared-component; all colors via CSS custom properties from `index.css`; no module-specific token overrides | Inline-styles; all color references via CSS custom properties from app-shell token registry; no module-specific token declarations | Inline-styles; all color references are CSS custom property calls into app-shell token set defined in `index.css`; no module-specific token definitions | All three: primary color infrastructure is the 138-token registry in `index.css`; no module owns its own token namespace | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.css:19–295` (138 tokens); hardcoded values in `AlarmListPane.tsx`, `PointTablePane.tsx`, `TrendPane.tsx`, `index.tsx` | `index.css:19–219`; `shared/theme/tokens.ts` (JS mirror); hardcoded overrides in `DesignerToolbar`, `DesignerTabBar`, `DesignerStatusBar`, `DesignerGraphicsList` | `index.css:17–219`; `settingsStyles.ts:1–71` (subset of token aliases); hardcoded overrides in `Users.tsx`, `Roles.tsx`, `Groups.tsx`, `OpcSources.tsx`, `BulkUpdate.tsx` | `index.css` is the canonical token registry for all three | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | ~20 token references; hardcoded hex for alarm priority/quality badge colors, TrendPane series palette, published dot | ~15 token references; 9 hardcoded color values in shell components; 2 undefined tokens (`--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`) | ~15 token references including `--io-border-subtle`, `--io-success`, `--io-status-fg`; undefined `--io-text-on-accent` in `btnPrimary`; hex-alpha badge concatenation bug in OpcSources | All three use `var(--io-accent)`, `var(--io-border)`, `var(--io-text-primary)`, `var(--io-text-muted)`, `var(--io-surface)` family, `var(--io-success)`/`--io-warning`/`--io-danger` | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | `var(--io-bg)` and `var(--io-text)` referenced but not defined in `index.css`; alarm priority badge colors hardcoded instead of using `--io-alarm-*` tokens; published dot `#10b981` ≠ `var(--io-success)` | `var(--io-surface-hover)` and `var(--io-font-sans)` undefined; 9 hardcoded values bypass tokens; `#09090b` hardcoded for active IconBtn text instead of `var(--io-accent-foreground)` | `--io-text-on-accent` undefined in `btnPrimary` across ~15 files; hex-alpha CSS var concatenation bug (`${color}20`) renders OpcSources StatusBadge backgrounds as invalid; hardcoded modal overlay/shadow values in Users/Roles/Groups | All three reference at least one undefined token; all three have hardcoded hex colors that bypass the token system | Each module references a different set of undefined tokens |
| **Notes** | Console quality badge colors (`#10B981`, `#F59E0B`, `#EF4444`) have no corresponding tokens; hardcoded is the only option for that sub-type | `DesignerGraphicsList` `SCOPE_COLORS`/`MODE_COLORS` are fully hardcoded rgba objects with no registry equivalent | OpcSources StatusBadge `${color}20` concatenation bug is a functional regression: backgrounds render as transparent in all browsers | — | — |

---

## Category 2 — Typography

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Inline-styles; no Console-specific CSS classes or shared typography component | Inline-styles; no module-local CSS; `FieldLabel` component in `DesignerRightPanel` is the only reusable label primitive in any Designer file | Inline-styles; no module-specific CSS; `settingsStyles.ts:labelStyle` exports a named label constant used across most pages | All three use inline-styles exclusively; no shared typography component exists across modules | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.css:179–218` (16 scale tokens + `--io-font-mono`); inline styles scattered across `index.tsx`, `PaneWrapper`, `ConsolePalette`, `PaneConfigModal`, `AlarmListPane` | `index.css:178–218` (same 16 tokens); `DesignerRightPanel:201–217` (`FieldLabel`); `DesignerModeTabs`, `DesignerToolbar`, `DesignerTabBar`, `DesignerStatusBar` | `index.css`; `index.tsx:198–224` (nav group/item); `SettingsPageLayout:36–55` (page title); `settingsStyles.ts:15–21` (labelStyle); `SettingsTabs:60–75` (tab labels) | `index.css` typography token scale is the reference for all three; none actually use it | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | Raw px integers 9–18px; `fontFamily: monospace` on alarm tag/time (not `--io-font-mono`); SubGroupLabel at 9px and PaneTypeBadge at 10px are below smallest token (11px) | Raw px integers 9–20px; `var(--io-font-mono)` used correctly for zoom input (only instance); `var(--io-font-sans)` referenced in zoom dropdown but undefined; RightPanel `FieldLabel`: 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em | Raw px integers 11–18px; bare `"monospace"` keyword in `Groups.tsx` (not `--io-font-mono`); `labelStyle`: 12px/500/`--io-text-secondary`; page title 18px/600; table headers 11px/600/uppercase | All three use raw pixel integer literals; none reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`); all inherit the browser sans-serif stack | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | Zero typography scale tokens used; `fontFamily: monospace` instead of `--io-font-mono`; SubGroupLabel 9px below minimum token; no font sizes map cleanly to token equivalents | Zero typography scale tokens used; `var(--io-font-sans)` undefined; five different `letterSpacing` values for uppercase labels within `DesignerRightPanel` alone (0em, 0.04em, 0.05em, 0.06em, 0.08em) | Nav group header 11px vs AppShell 10px (1px drift); three different `letterSpacing` values across semantically identical uppercase label elements (0.06em, 0.07em, 0.08em); bare `"monospace"` in `Groups.tsx` instead of `--io-font-mono` | All three: zero typography scale tokens used; all three use bare `monospace` or no font-family token for monospace contexts | — |
| **Notes** | None of the inline sizes map cleanly to token equivalents (e.g., 15px, 16px are not defined tokens) | `FieldLabel` (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em) is the only reusable typography primitive in any of the three modules | Typography is most internally consistent in Settings; deviations are minor relative to Console and Designer | — | — |

---

## Category 3 — Toolbars

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Module-local-component; single 48px horizontal header bar defined as inline JSX inside `ConsolePage` | Module-local-component; four stacked dedicated components (`DesignerModeTabs` 36px, `DesignerToolbar` 44px, `DesignerTabBar` 36px, `DesignerStatusBar` 28px) | **Absent.** Per-page action rows above tables are inline flexbox rows, not toolbars. No toolbar container, no icon-button strip found | N/A — Settings has no toolbars; Console and Designer both use module-local components with no shared toolbar primitive | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.tsx:1942–2826` (full header + tab strip); `index.tsx:247–466` (`LayoutPickerButton`) | `DesignerModeTabs:207–217`; `DesignerToolbar:1159–1169`; `DesignerTabBar:363–376`; `DesignerStatusBar:192–203` | N/A | — | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | 48px height; `background: var(--io-surface)`; `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; active tab: `borderBottom: 2px solid var(--io-accent)`; tab labels 13px/600 active/400 inactive; separator dividers `width:1, height:18, background: var(--io-border)` | DesignerToolbar 44px: same bg/border pattern; `IconBtn` 32×32 `var(--io-radius)`, `transition: background 0.1s, color 0.1s`; mode/tab strips with 2px accent underline; StatusBar 28px at bottom with segment dividers | N/A | Console + Designer share: `background: var(--io-surface)`, `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, active tab underline `2px solid var(--io-accent)`, tab labels font-weight active/inactive distinction | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | `var(--io-surface)` background where app shell topbar uses `var(--io-surface-primary)` (darker); none of 6 `--io-btn-*` tokens used; no toolbar height token; Publish button rendered twice in right controls block (duplicate render) | Toolbar text action buttons use `borderRadius: 6` integer while IconBtn in the same component uses `var(--io-radius)` (both 6px currently but inconsistent); all heights hardcoded (36/44/36/28); loading skeleton uses 40px instead of actual 44px | N/A | Console + Designer: all toolbar heights are hardcoded integers with no token | — |
| **Notes** | `LayoutPickerButton` is module-local with its own custom inline absolute-positioned dropdown (`zIndex: 200`) | Loading skeleton toolbar placeholder uses 40px height vs the actual 44px, producing a brief layout shift on load | N/A | — | — |

---

## Category 4 — Menus

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Mix; right-click context menus use `shared/components/ContextMenu`; export quick-format dropdown is a hand-rolled inline-positioned panel | Mix; three patterns: custom inline dropdown (File menu, `position: absolute`), custom fixed-position dropdown (zoom presets, `getBoundingClientRect`), shared `ContextMenu` (tab right-click) | Shared-component only; `ContextMenu` is the sole menu type; no custom dropdown panels | All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` for right-click context menus | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.tsx:3108–3405` (right-click menus); `index.tsx:2591–2654` (export dropdown); `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | `DesignerModeTabs:317–389` (File menu); `DesignerToolbar:1373–1469` (zoom dropdown); `DesignerTabBar:102–253` (tab context menu); `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx`; `Users:1628–1660`, `Roles`, `Groups` | `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | ContextMenu: delegated to shared component; Export dropdown: `position: absolute`, `background: var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `border: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: 6`, `boxShadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)`, items 13px | ContextMenu: delegated to shared component; File menu: `position: absolute`, `var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `boxShadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.35)`, `zIndex: 300`; Zoom dropdown: `position: fixed` (anchored via `getBoundingClientRect`), `zIndex: 2000` | ContextMenu container: `var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `border: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius)`, `boxShadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)`, `zIndex: 1800`; items: `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger (not `--io-danger`) | All three: ContextMenu uses `var(--io-surface-elevated)` bg, `var(--io-border)` border, `var(--io-radius)` radius; hover applied via imperative `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` DOM mutation | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | Export dropdown uses direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.background`) for hover rather than shared `ContextMenu`; no CSS class or React state | Zoom dropdown hover uses `var(--io-surface-hover)` (undefined token — no visual feedback); zoom font uses `var(--io-font-sans)` (undefined); box-shadows differ between File menu and zoom dropdown and do not use `--io-shadow-lg`; `zIndex: 300` vs `zIndex: 2000` across two custom dropdowns | None; Settings consumes `ContextMenu` identically to other modules | All three: custom dropdown implementations (where present) do not use `--io-shadow-lg` token | ContextMenu uses `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger items rather than `var(--io-danger)`; same numeric value in dark theme but diverges in light theme |
| **Notes** | Export dropdown backdrop uses `position: fixed` — safe here since toolbar is not inside an RGL transform ancestor | File menu has no Escape key handler; Zoom dropdown uses `position: fixed` to escape toolbar's potential transform context (consistent with CLAUDE.md invariant) | `ConfirmDialog` (shared component) is used correctly for destructive actions; no dropdown menus needed | — | — |

---

## Category 5 — Side Panels

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Module-local-component; `ConsolePalette.tsx` with `useConsolePanelResize` hook; 220px default width, resizable by drag | Module-local-component; four files (`DesignerLeftPalette`, `DesignerRightPanel`, `ShapePointSelector`, `PointPickerModal`); no shared panel primitive | Module-local-component; bespoke `<aside>` in `index.tsx`; not derived from app-shell sidebar infrastructure | None; all three implement side panels independently | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `ConsolePalette.tsx:52–537`; `index.tsx:1127–1132` (state); `useConsolePanelResize.ts` | `DesignerLeftPalette:2430–2438`; `DesignerRightPanel:5961–5992`; `ShapePointSelector:378–393`; `PointPickerModal:44–55` | `index.tsx:179–229` (aside); `AppShell.tsx:1327–1516` (comparison baseline) | — | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | 220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`; `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; accordion section headers 36px height; section label 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; `--io-text-muted`; resize handle 5px `ns-resize` | DesignerLeftPalette: `background: var(--io-surface)`, `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; `SectionHeader` 11px/600/uppercase/0.06em; 4px resize handle; palette tiles `var(--io-surface-elevated)` bg; DesignerRightPanel: `background: var(--io-surface)`, `borderLeft: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; RightPanel TabBar tabs 10px/uppercase | 220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`; `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; nav items `padding: 7px 10px`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius)`; active: `var(--io-accent-subtle)` bg + `var(--io-accent)` text/color | Console + Settings: 220px width, `var(--io-surface-secondary)` bg, `borderRight: 1px solid var(--io-border)` | DesignerLeftPalette diverges: uses `var(--io-surface)` not `var(--io-surface-secondary)` |
| **Deviations from app shell** | 220px vs `--io-sidebar-width: 240px`; `var(--io-text)` undefined in section search input; view mode selector hover via DOM mutation (`borderRadius: 3` vs `var(--io-radius)` on list items); `--io-sidebar-bg` token not used | DesignerLeftPalette: equipment tiles use `var(--io-surface-sunken)` vs `var(--io-surface-elevated)` for other tile types; drag ghost hardcodes `borderRadius: 4px` and `color: "#09090b"`; `CanvasLayerRow` emoji icons (👁/🔒) vs SVG elsewhere; `var(--io-surface-raised)` undefined for `RowSection`; all hover via DOM mutation | 220px vs `--io-sidebar-width: 240px`; active left-border accent missing (AppShell shows `2px solid var(--io-accent)` on active items; Settings shows none); no hover transition (AppShell transitions bg/color); group header 11px vs AppShell 10px | Console + Settings: same width deviation (220px vs 240px token) and same background token (`var(--io-surface-secondary)`) but neither uses `--io-sidebar-width` | — |
| **Notes** | `PointsBrowserPanel` (app-shell) embedded inside the palette Points section; its styles are app-shell-owned | Stencil tile uses ⬜ emoji placeholder thumbnail; equipment tiles use `ShapeThumbnail` SVG; display element tiles use `DisplayElementPreview` SVG — three thumbnail strategies | Missing active left-border accent is the most visible deviation from AppShell nav pattern | — | — |

---

## Category 6 — Buttons

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Inline-styles; all native `<button>` elements; no shared Button component | Mix; no shared `<Button>` component; five patterns: primary (filled accent), secondary (bordered), destructive, `IconBtn` primitive, toggle/chip; `IconBtn` is the only reusable button primitive | Module-local-component; four named variants in `settingsStyles.ts` (`btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall`); row action buttons in `Users.tsx` duplicated inline | All three use `var(--io-accent)` for primary action button backgrounds; all three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` for secondary/outline borders; none use the 6 `--io-btn-*` tokens | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.tsx:2116–2824`; `PaneWrapper:143–619`; `PaneConfigModal:461–492`; `AlarmListPane:176–192`; `PointTablePane:185–270` | `DesignerToolbar:904–948` (IconBtn), `1618–1693` (text actions); `DesignerTabBar:208–238`; `DesignerHome:55–84`; `DesignerGraphicsList:342–955`; `DesignerImport:64–90`; `index.tsx:290–450` | `settingsStyles.ts:23–63`; `Users.tsx:1462–1519`; `Groups.tsx:27–31` | — | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | Primary: `var(--io-accent)` bg, `#fff` text, `borderRadius: 6`, `padding: 7–9px 14–20px`; Secondary toolbar: transparent, `var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: 6`, 12px; Modal buttons: `borderRadius: 4`; Alarm Ack: `borderRadius: 3`; No hover states on toolbar or modal buttons | Primary: `var(--io-accent)` bg, `#fff` or `#09090b` text (hardcoded), `var(--io-radius)`; `IconBtn`: 32×32, `var(--io-radius)`, `transition: background 0.1s, color 0.1s`, hover `var(--io-surface-elevated)`; toolbar text actions: `borderRadius: 6` integer; tab close: `borderRadius: 3`; StatusBar segments are `<div>` not `<button>` | `btnPrimary`: `var(--io-accent)`, `var(--io-text-on-accent)` (undefined), `var(--io-radius)`, 13px/600; `btnSecondary`: transparent, `var(--io-text-secondary)`, `var(--io-border)`, `var(--io-radius)`, 13px; `btnDanger`: transparent, `var(--io-danger)`, `1px solid var(--io-danger)`, `var(--io-radius)`, 13px/600; no hover/focus styles on any variant | All three: primary button uses `var(--io-accent)` bg; secondary uses transparent bg + `var(--io-border)`; none use `--io-btn-*` tokens | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | None of the 6 `--io-btn-*` tokens used; `borderRadius` inconsistent (6/4/3); `#fff` hardcoded on primary instead of `var(--io-accent-foreground)`; no hover states on any toolbar, pane icon, or modal button; no `:focus-visible` ring on any button | `#fff` or `#09090b` hardcoded on primary (not `var(--io-accent-foreground)`); `DesignerImport` fallback `#3b82f6` (blue) on `btnPrimary` is wrong hue; toolbar text actions use `borderRadius: 6` integer while IconBtn uses `var(--io-radius)`; two incompatible destructive styles (ghost rgba vs solid fill) | `--io-text-on-accent` undefined in `btnPrimary`; `btnSecondary`/`btnSmall` have no `fontWeight` (browser default 400 vs 600 on primary/danger); row action buttons duplicated inline in `Users.tsx`; close icon inconsistency (`✕` vs `x`); no hover or focus styles on any variant | All three: primary button text color is hardcoded (`#fff` or `#09090b`) or uses undefined token (`--io-text-on-accent`) rather than `var(--io-accent-foreground)`; all three suppress or lack hover/focus styles | — |
| **Notes** | No `:focus-visible` ring on any button in Console | StatusBar clickable grid/zoom/binding-summary segments are `<div onClick>` not `<button>` — no keyboard navigation | `BulkUpdate.tsx` defines its own full button set (`BTN_PRIMARY`, `BTN_SECONDARY`, `BTN_DANGER`) independently from `settingsStyles`, introducing a second button system within Settings | — | — |

---

## Category 7 — Form Inputs

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules

1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
7. **`--io-input-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the five `--io-input-bg`, `--io-input-border`, `--io-input-focus-border`, `--io-input-placeholder`, `--io-input-height` tokens defined in `index.css`.
8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
11. **No `--io-modal-*` tokens used.** All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`, the three modal-specific tokens defined in `index.css`.
12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.

---

## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules

1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including two undefined tokens (`--io-modal-backdrop`, `--io-overlay`) with no consistent value among the three.
3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.
7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-info`, `--io-space-*`, `--io-accent-rgb`, `--io-surface`.
8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — all uncoordinated with the `--io-z-modal: 300` token.
12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.

---

## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on

1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.

2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.

3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.

4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.

5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.

6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.

7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.

# UI Audit Verification — Code-Level Fact-Check

**Source verified against:** live frontend source code only. No design docs or audit files consulted.
**Date:** 2026-05-27

---

## Verification Method

Every claim in the 11 category tables (5 rows each × 11 categories = 55 table rows) and 3 lists (12 + 12 + 7 = 31 items) was checked by reading the cited source files. Source of truth is the code. Comparison file (`02-comparison.md`) is not amended.

---

## Table of Results

| All | Cat 1 | Source-of-truth files | "`index.css:19–295` (138 tokens)" | YES | The comment in index.css itself states 138 tokens; the range 19–295 reaches through the light-theme pen colors, which is correct for Console. Designer/Settings ranges (19–219 and 17–219) correspond to the dark-theme block end and the block opening comment; minor variation but not wrong. |
| All | Cat 1 | Visual properties | "~20 token references; hardcoded hex for alarm priority/quality badge colors, TrendPane series palette, published dot" | YES | Confirmed: `#10b981` published dot in console/index.tsx:2034; PRIORITY_COLOR hardcoded in AlarmListPane.tsx:34-35; QualityBadge hardcoded in PointTablePane.tsx:30-34. |
| Designer | Cat 1 | Notes | "SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS are **fully hardcoded** rgba objects with no registry equivalent" | **NO** | `SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"` (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48) and `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"` (line 55). Two of the six entries use registered tokens. Not "fully hardcoded." |
| All | Cat 1 | Deviations | "`var(--io-bg)` and `var(--io-text)` referenced but not defined in `index.css`" | YES | Confirmed: neither `--io-bg` nor bare `--io-text` appears in index.css. Both are genuinely undefined. |

---

## Discrepancy Summary

11 discrepancies found in the comparison file. Each is a claim that the code contradicts:

1. **Cat 1 Notes (Designer)** — SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS: claimed "fully hardcoded" but two entries reference `var(--io-accent)` and `var(--io-text-secondary)`. (`DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55`)

2. **Cat 7 Visual Properties (Settings)** — `labelStyle`: claimed "uppercase/0.05em" but actual code has neither `textTransform: "uppercase"` nor `letterSpacing`. (`settingsStyles.ts:15-21`)

3. **Cat 7 Deviations (Designer)** — `--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border` in ShapePointSelector: claimed "appear to be unregistered." Both ARE registered in index.css (lines 131-132). (`ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367`)

4. **Cat 7 Deviations (Settings)** — `--io-space-*`: claimed "appear unregistered." Seventeen `--io-space-*` tokens ARE defined in index.css:148-164.

5. **Cat 8 Deviations (Settings)** — `--io-info` in AuthProviders TypeBadge: claimed "undefined." `--io-info: #3b82f6` IS defined in index.css:72. Fallback value equals token value; no regression exists. (`AuthProviders.tsx:528`)

6. **Cat 10 Notes (Designer)** — `io-multiselect-active`: claimed "no observable CSS rule." Rule exists at index.css:904-909 (`svg.io-multiselect-active [data-node-id].io-selected { filter: drop-shadow(…) }`).

7. **Cat 11 Deviations (Settings)** — native confirm() count: claimed "4 calls." Actual count is **8 calls** across 4 files: OpcSources(3), Import(3), CameraStreams(1), SupplementalConnectorsTab(1).

8. **Cat 11 Visual Properties (Settings)** / **List 2 Item 7** — `--io-modal-backdrop`: claimed "undefined." `--io-modal-backdrop: var(--io-surface-overlay)` IS defined in index.css:139 and used in ≥6 Settings files.

9. **List 2 Item 7 (Settings)** — `--io-info`: claimed undefined. IS defined (see #5 above).

10. **List 2 Item 7 (Settings)** — `--io-space-*`: claimed undefined. IS defined (see #4 above).

11. **List 2 Item 7 (Settings)** — `--io-surface`: claimed undefined. `--io-surface: var(--io-surface-elevated)` IS defined in index.css:29.

---

## Missing-from-Comparison Entries

4 elements exist in code but have no entry in the comparison:

1. **`shared/clipboard/selection/selection.css` + `MarqueeLayer.tsx`** — Both reference `var(--accent)` (without the `--io-` prefix), a token not defined anywhere in the codebase. `selection.css` lines 2 and 9 use it for selection box outline (`2px solid var(--accent)`) and glow (`box-shadow: … var(--accent)`). `MarqueeLayer.tsx:101` uses `border: "1px dashed var(--accent)"`. With no definition, all three resolve to the CSS initial value, meaning selection highlight and marquee border have no visible color. Should be `var(--io-accent)`.

2. **`shared/graphics/alarmFlash.css`** — CSS keyframe animations for alarm flash states (`io-alarm-flash-urgent`, `io-alarm-flash-high`, etc.) use hardcoded hex colors: `#ef4444`, `#f97316`, `#eab308`, `#f4f4f5`, `#60a5fa` rather than `--io-alarm-*` tokens. These animations apply to display elements in the graphics canvas but do not adapt to light or HPHMI themes. The comparison covers alarm badge colors in Cat 1 and 8 but does not mention this file.

3. **`shared/graphics/operationalState.css`** — ISA-101 equipment operational state CSS classes (`io-running`, `io-stopped`, `io-fault`, `io-transitioning`, `io-oos`) apply hardcoded hex fills and strokes (`#047857`, `#808080`, `#dc2626`, `#d97706`) to SVG elements via `!important` overrides. No token system used. Not mentioned in the comparison under any category.

4. **`shared/graphics/lod.css`** — Level-of-detail visibility rules for graphics elements. Structural CSS only (opacity transitions, `display: none` for non-adjacent LOD levels). No color tokens used. Not mentioned in the comparison; relevant to Canvas / Main Work Area (Cat 10) as a separate style layer governing display element visibility.

---

## Totals

- **Rows checked:** 86 (55 table rows + 31 list items)
- **Discrepancies found:** 11
- **Missing-from-comparison entries:** 4
