## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules

1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
7. **`--io-input-*` tokens largely unused.** Console and Settings ignore all five `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's `ShapePointSelector` uses `--io-input-bg` (index.css:131) and `--io-input-border` (index.css:132). Three of the five tokens remain unused across all three modules.
8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
11. **`--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused.** `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are ignored by all three modules; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer do not use it.
12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.

---

## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules

1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.
3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.
7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29).
8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — all uncoordinated with the `--io-z-modal: 300` token.
12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.

---

## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on

1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.

2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.

3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.

4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.

5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.

6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.

7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.

+| Field | operationalState.css |

+| **Implementation** | ISA-101 operational state CSS classes applied as class names to symbol `<g>` wrapper elements. Five mutually exclusive states: `io-running`, `io-stopped`, `io-fault`, `io-transitioning`, `io-oos`. Uses `!important` overrides to guarantee state classes override shape-specific SVG attributes set at authoring time. One `@keyframes` animation (`io-state-transitioning-pulse`) and one `@supports` OOS hatch fallback. |
+| **Source-of-truth files** | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/operationalState.css:1-73` |

+| **Visual properties actually applied** | `io-running`: stroke `#047857 !important` (emerald-700), circle fill `rgba(5,150,105,0.15) !important`; `io-stopped`: stroke `#808080 !important`; `io-fault`: stroke `#dc2626 !important`, circle fill `rgba(220,38,38,0.12) !important`; `io-transitioning`: stroke `#d97706 !important` + `opacity 1→0.5→1, 1.5s ease-in-out infinite` pulse; `io-oos`: stroke `#808080 !important` + hatch fill |

+| **Deviations from app shell** | All colors hardcoded hex with `!important` overrides. **Documented exception — intentional design, not a gap to close:** `!important` is required to override SVG attribute-level fill/stroke set in authored symbol geometry. ISA-101 prescribes specific operational state colors that intentionally differ from project tokens (e.g., `#047857` emerald-700 running vs `--io-alarm-normal: #22c55e` green-500 — different by standard; `#dc2626` fault vs `--io-alarm-urgent: #ef4444` — physically distinct semantic, different value by design). This file is intentionally non-token-driven for ISA-101 compliance. |

+| **Notes** | The hardcoded ISA-101 colors with `!important` are the correct and intentional implementation. Do not treat as a gap to close in the recommendations phase. Consumed by SceneRenderer assigning state classes based on bound point operational state values. Primary: Cat 10. Secondary: Cat 8. |

+| Field | lod.css |

+| **Implementation** | Level-of-Detail (LOD) visibility rules for the graphics canvas. Canvas container receives one of four classes (`lod-0` through `lod-3`); SVG elements carry `data-lod` attributes. Controls visibility via opacity and display at zoom thresholds: LOD 0 (< 15%, overview), LOD 1 (15–40%, area), LOD 2 (40–80%, unit), LOD 3 (> 80%, detail). |
+| **Source-of-truth files** | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/lod.css:1-67` |

+| **Visual properties actually applied** | Default: `transition: opacity 200ms ease` on all `[data-lod]` elements; adjacent tier: `opacity: 0, pointer-events: none`; non-adjacent tier: `display: none`; LOD 3: `opacity: 1, pointer-events: auto`. No color properties, no CSS custom properties, no hardcoded hex. |
+| **Deviations from app shell** | None. Structural-only CSS (opacity, display, pointer-events, transition timing). Fully compliant with project token system by virtue of using no color properties. |

+| **Notes** | Recorded for completeness — lod.css governs an important canvas behavior layer but has no token deviations to address. `display: none` for non-adjacent tiers reduces GPU compositing cost at high element counts. LOD class applied to `io-canvas-container` div; zoom-to-LOD logic lives in SceneRenderer/GraphicPane. Consumed exclusively by the graphics canvas. |

4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.

6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.

-7. **`--io-input-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the five `--io-input-bg`, `--io-input-border`, `--io-input-focus-border`, `--io-input-placeholder`, `--io-input-height` tokens defined in `index.css`.

+7. **`--io-input-*` tokens largely unused.** Console and Settings ignore all five `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's `ShapePointSelector` uses `--io-input-bg` (index.css:131) and `--io-input-border` (index.css:132). Three of the five tokens remain unused across all three modules.

8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.

9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.

10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
-11. **No `--io-modal-*` tokens used.** All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`, the three modal-specific tokens defined in `index.css`.

+11. **`--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused.** `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are ignored by all three modules; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer do not use it.

12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.
## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules
1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.

-2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including two undefined tokens (`--io-modal-backdrop`, `--io-overlay`) with no consistent value among the three.

+2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.

3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.

4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.

5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).

6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.

-7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-info`, `--io-space-*`, `--io-accent-rgb`, `--io-surface`.

+7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29).

8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.

10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.

6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.

7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.
